description:
You are a Coast Guard operations officer at MRCC Falmouth (Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre) creating a Search and Rescue (SAR) training exercise for coordinating vessel response in storm conditions off the Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall. Build a complete SAR scenario at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/p) Lizard SAR Exercise Storm and prepare a supporting briefing document. 

Configure the environment (environment.ini) with Setting='Falmouth', StartTime=3.0, Weather=8.0, Rain=5.0, VisibilityRange=3.0, Variation=-2.5, StartMonth=1, and StartYear=2025. Set the ownship (ownship.ini) as an 'RNLI Severn Class' positioned at 49.96°N, 5.22°W, heading 045 at 15 knots with GPS and depth sounder enabled. Populate the scenario (othership.ini) with exactly six vessels, each having 2-3 waypoint legs: a 'Fishing Vessel' (casualty) drifting at 1-2 knots near 49.98°N, 5.18°W; a 'Tanker' heading 070 at 12 kts; a 'Container Ship' heading 250 at 14 kts; a 'Patrol' vessel heading toward the casualty at 18 kts; a 'Yacht' (distress) drifting at 1 knot; and a 'Cargo' vessel heading toward the casualty area at 10 kts. Configure system settings (bc5.ini) with arpa_on=1, full_radar=1, radar_range_resolution=512, radar_angular_resolution=720, max_radar_range=96, and hide_instruments=0. 

Create a SAR briefing of at least 25 lines at /home/ga/Documents/sar_briefing.txt containing an incident summary, casualty description, datum point, search pattern assignment, vessel roles, communication plan (VHF channels 16 and 67), weather forecast, and safety warnings. Login: ga / password123.
